data.title: Add Refusal Gate section to preplanning SKILL.md for missing/incomplete External Dependencies block
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## TDD Requirement
Task 1 (bb8f-cde2) wrote a failing structural test: test_refusal_gate_section_heading_exists. This task makes that test GREEN by adding the Refusal Gate section to SKILL.md.

## Implementation Notes

### What to add

Add a **Refusal Gate** section immediately before Phase 3 (Walking Skeleton & Vertical Slicing), after Phase 2.5 (Adversarial Review). The section activates only when EXTERNAL_DEP_BLOCK_ENABLED=true (set by Phase 1.5 flag check).

The Refusal Gate:
1. Scans all drafted Success Criteria from the parent epic for externally-shaped SCs (SCs whose outcomes are observable only in deployed/external contexts, as detected by brainstorm's shape heuristic in story 72e9-d6c8)
2. For each externally-shaped SC found, checks whether the parent epic's External Dependencies block contains a valid entry for that SC
3. If ANY externally-shaped SC lacks a matching block entry (block missing or entry incomplete per the contract schema from bec5-7f84):
   - HALT decomposition
   - Emit a diagnostic that names the specific SC(s) that are externally-shaped but have no block coverage
   - Direct the user to run /dso:brainstorm <epic-id> to capture the External Dependencies block
   - Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 story creation
4. If all externally-shaped SCs have valid block entries (or there are no externally-shaped SCs): proceed to Phase 3 normally

### Diagnostic format
"Preplanning cannot decompose this epic: the following success criteria are externally-shaped but have no corresponding entry in the External Dependencies block: [list of SC names]. Run `/dso:brainstorm <epic-id>` to capture the dependency information, then retry `/dso:preplanning`."

### Section heading
`## Refusal Gate: External Dependencies Block Check (/dso:preplanning)`

### Behavioral testing standard constraint
SKILL.md is a non-executable LLM instruction file. The structural test from Task 1 verifies only that the section heading exists. Behavioral correctness of the refusal logic (LLM detecting externally-shaped SCs and emitting the right diagnostic) is probabilistic and not deterministically testable — correct per Rule 5.

### Scope boundary
This refusal gate fires ONLY when planning.external_dependency_block_enabled=true (set in Phase 1.5). When the flag is off, the gate never executes. This preserves DD4 (flag off = baseline behavior).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SKILL.md contains Refusal Gate section heading
  Verify: grep -q 'Refusal Gate' plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md
- [ ] Refusal Gate section appears before Phase 3 in SKILL.md
  Verify: python3 -c "text=open('plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md').read(); rg=text.find('Refusal Gate'); p3=text.find('## Phase 3:'); exit(0 if 0 < rg < p3 else 1)"
- [ ] Refusal Gate diagnostic references /dso:brainstorm in SKILL.md
  Verify: grep -q '/dso:brainstorm' plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md
- [ ] All Task 1 structural tests now pass (GREEN) — including test_refusal_gate_section_heading_exists
  Verify: bash plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/tests/test_external_dep_block_reader.sh; test $? -eq 0
- [ ] validate.sh passes (no new shim violations or referential integrity breaks)
  Verify: bash .claude/scripts/dso validate.sh --ci 2>/dev/null | tail -5
- [ ] No new external file references in SKILL.md that do not resolve
  Verify: bash plugins/dso/hooks/check-referential-integrity.sh plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md 2>/dev/null; test $? -eq 0
